title: Mailbox Heist
summary: An EML hides a compressed attachment that must be extracted from the raw message source.
scenario: An email claims a vendor packet was resent as a bundled attachment. Solvers need to inspect the raw message, recover the MIME attachment without relying on a mail client, and extract the archived content to obtain the flag.
learningObjectives: Recover a binary attachment from a raw email artifact
Inspect a recovered archive without relying on GUI mail tools

artifacts.name: target_<seed>.eml
artifacts.purpose: Generated email artifact containing an archived attachment

artifacts.name: triage_note.txt
artifacts.purpose: Short analyst note describing the suspicious message

hints: The useful evidence is in the attachment, not the visible message body.
Treat the EML as structured source data, not just something to open in a mail client.

llmHandoff.scope: This folder contains the metadata, generator, and internal notes for the challenge.
llmHandoff.implementNext: Tune the email narrative, attachment naming, or extraction hints while preserving the raw-message solve path.
llmHandoff.doNotDoYet: Do not expose the attachment contents or real flag in plaintext portal assets.
